prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "斜上俯卧撑" (incline push-up) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of an incline push-up (hands elevated on a knee-height bench/box): hands on top of a bench shoulder-width apart; toes on the ground further away from the bench so body is at a roughly 30-45 degree angle to the floor; arms bent so chest approaches the bench; body in one straight line from heels to head; this is an easier scaled variant of push-up emphasizing the lower chest; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: pectoralis major (especially the lower fibers) and triceps. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: anterior deltoid and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "胸大肌" pointing to the pectoralis major (lower fibers); "肱三头肌" pointing to the triceps; "三角肌前束 / 核心" pointing to the anterior deltoid and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
